tool.name: merger
tool.summary: Merge two overlapping sequences by longest exact suffix/prefix overlap

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `merger` combines exactly one sequence record from a left input and one sequence record from a right input by taking the longest positive exact overlap between the left suffix and right prefix. The Epithema v1 implementation is intentionally conservative and emits one merged sequence record rather than attempting assembly graph or consensus behavior.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts two local sequence inputs. Each input must contain exactly one sequence record. The two records must have the same inferred molecule kind. If no positive exact overlap exists, the tool fails instead of concatenating the sequences blindly.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits one merged FASTA sequence record through the shared result path. CLI summaries report the left and right source inputs, the overlap length used, the exact-overlap merge rule, and FASTA output format.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema merger`. Validation currently covers a deterministic one-record overlap merge against committed FASTA fixtures, as well as no-overlap failure behavior in the Rust tool layer.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release does not attempt approximate overlap detection, reverse-complement searching, quality-aware merging, or feature-coordinate reconciliation. It uses only exact left-suffix/right-prefix overlap and carries forward left-side metadata without remapping features.

artifacts.label: Left overlap FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed left input fixture ending with the exact overlap used for deterministic merger validation.

artifacts.label: Right overlap FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed right input fixture beginning with the exact overlap used for deterministic merger validation.

examples.title: Merge two one-record FASTA inputs by exact overlap
examples.description: Runs `merger` against committed left and right FASTA fixtures and emits one merged sequence record using the longest exact overlap.

expected_outputs.label: Merged sequence record
expected_outputs.description: A FASTA sequence record with residues `ACGTAAGGG` and an overlap length of `3` reported in the CLI summary.
